wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AIPulse project overview section focused on introducing the AI Tool Manager application. Explain its purpose as a personal AI tool collection management system, core value proposition for organizing and discovering AI tools, and target audience including AI enthusiasts and professionals. Document the key features including CRUD operations, category-based organization, search functionality, drag-and-drop reordering, theme switching, and recently used tools tracking. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners explaining what AIPulse solves and technical details for experienced developers covering the modern React + TypeScript architecture. Use terminology consistent with the codebase such as 'AITool', 'category management', and 'state persistence'. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like adding AI tools, organizing collections, and discovering new tools. Document the application's role in the AI ecosystem and its relationship to external AI tool URLs and browser integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Store Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the Zustand store architecture in AIPulse. Document the createStore pattern implementation, state shape definition using ToolsState interface, and action creator organization. Explain the store initialization with default values including tools array, categories array, searchQuery, selectedCategory, theme preference, and recentlyUsed tracking. Detail the action patterns for CRUD operations (addTool, updateTool, deleteTool), category management (addCategory, deleteCategory), filter operations (setSearchQuery, setSelectedCategory), theme operations (toggleTheme, setDarkMode), and recently used tracking (addToRecentlyUsed). Include the store configuration with persist middleware setup, state partitioning strategy, and performance optimization techniques. Provide examples of store usage patterns, action dispatching, and state subscription methods.

wiki_catalogs.name: CRUD Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for the CRUD operations in AIPulse tool management system. Document the complete Create operation workflow including ToolModal form submission, validation rules, category assignment, and icon selection process. Explain the Read operation implementation showing tool display in ToolGrid and individual ToolCard rendering. Detail the Update operation covering edit modal functionality, real-time validation, change tracking, and state persistence. Document the Delete operation including confirmation dialogs, cascade effects, and data cleanup procedures. Include form validation strategies, error handling mechanisms, and user feedback systems. Provide examples of each operation with code snippets from ToolModal and toolsStore implementations. Address data integrity maintenance, duplicate detection, and validation failure scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Theme System & Color Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AIPulse theme system implementation. Document the dark/light mode toggle component including Lucide React icon switching, state management through Zustand store, and DOM class manipulation for theme application. Explain the Tailwind CSS dark mode configuration, color scheme variables, and system preference detection. Detail the theme persistence mechanism, theme transition animations using Framer Motion, and accessibility considerations for theme switching. Include implementation patterns for customizing colors, adding new themes, and maintaining visual consistency across components. Document the theme-aware styling approach and how components adapt to different color schemes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layout Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AIPulse layout components focusing on Header and Footer implementations. Document the Header component's role in navigation, search functionality, and theme switching. Explain the Footer component's structure and positioning within the application layout. Detail component props, event handlers, and integration with global state management. Include styling approaches using Tailwind CSS, responsive design patterns, and accessibility features. Document component composition patterns and how layout components wrap the main application content. Provide examples of customizing header navigation items and footer content. Address component lifecycle management and performance considerations for layout rendering.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started documentation for AIPulse development environment setup. Cover prerequisites including Node.js version requirements, npm/yarn package manager basics, and system requirements. Document step-by-step installation instructions starting from repository cloning, dependency installation using 'npm install' or 'yarn install', and development server startup with 'npm run dev'. Explain the development workflow including hot reloading, TypeScript compilation, and Vite build process. Include basic usage examples showing how to add the first AI tool, navigate categories, and use search functionality. Document common development commands including 'npm run build' for production builds and 'npm run preview' for local testing. Address initial configuration steps such as understanding the project structure, key directories, and development tools. Provide troubleshooting guidance for common setup issues and environment-specific considerations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AIPulse data models and TypeScript interfaces. Document the AITool interface including properties like id, name, description, url, category, icon, createdAt, lastAccessed, and order. Explain the Category interface structure with id and name properties. Detail the ToolsState interface covering tools array, categories array, searchQuery string, selectedCategory nullable, isDarkMode boolean, and recentlyUsed string array. Document the PersistedState interface used for localStorage serialization. Include the defaultTools and defaultCategories constants, their structure, and initialization patterns. Provide examples of data validation, type safety enforcement, and interface usage throughout the application. Address data transformation patterns, type guards, and optional property handling.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Lifecycle Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for tool lifecycle management in AIPulse. Document the complete tool journey from creation through deletion including state transitions, metadata updates, and persistence cycles. Explain tool initialization with default values, validation during creation, and state updates during editing. Detail the tool launch process including recently used tracking, URL validation, and external integration. Document tool organization features such as drag-and-drop reordering, category changes, and search indexing. Include tool metadata management covering descriptions, URLs, icons, and categorization. Address tool validation throughout lifecycle including URL format checking, duplicate prevention, and category constraints. Provide examples of tool state management, event handling, and user interaction patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Responsive Design & Layout
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for AIPulse responsive design architecture. Document the mobile-first approach using Tailwind CSS utility classes, breakpoint management, and adaptive grid layouts. Explain the header navigation behavior across screen sizes, category tabs responsiveness, and tool grid adaptation. Detail the flexbox and grid layout systems, spacing utilities, and typography scaling. Include guidelines for maintaining visual hierarchy on different devices, touch-friendly interaction patterns, and performance optimization for mobile experiences. Document the responsive breakpoints, media query patterns, and component adaptation strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Feature Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for AIPulse feature components that implement core application functionality. Document CategoryTabs for tool categorization and filtering, ToolGrid for displaying and organizing tools in a responsive grid, SearchBar for tool discovery, ThemeToggle for dark/light mode switching, RecentlyUsed for tracking tool usage, and ToolCard for individual tool representation. Explain component interfaces, state management integration with Zustand store, and user interaction patterns. Detail drag-and-drop functionality implementation using @dnd-kit/core, animation systems with Framer Motion, and responsive design considerations. Include examples of component customization, event handling patterns, and integration with external APIs. Document component composition patterns and how features work together to create cohesive user experiences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack & Dependencies
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive technology stack documentation for AIPulse covering the modern frontend development ecosystem. Document the core frameworks including React 19.2.4 as the component library, TypeScript 5.9.3 for type safety, and Vite 7.3.1 as the build tool. Detail key dependencies including Zustand 5.0.11 for state management, @dnd-kit/core 6.3.1 for drag-and-drop functionality, Framer Motion 12.35.0 for animations, and Lucide React 0.577.0 for icons. Explain Tailwind CSS 4.2.1 configuration for utility-first styling and dark mode implementation. Document development dependencies including React TypeScript types, PostCSS, autoprefixer, and build-time tools. Include version compatibility notes, upgrade paths, and rationale for technology choices. Address build configuration aspects including path aliases, TypeScript compilation settings, and Tailwind CSS customization. Provide guidance on dependency management and potential alternatives for each technology choice.

wiki_catalogs.name: Persistence Strategy
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for the Zustand persistence middleware implementation and data persistence strategy. Document the persist middleware configuration including storage key 'aipulse-storage', state partitioning with partialize function, and automatic synchronization with localStorage. Explain the PersistedState interface mapping and selective state persistence approach. Detail the state serialization and deserialization processes, including data transformation during storage and retrieval. Document the state hydration process on application startup, initial state fallback mechanisms, and data migration strategies. Include error handling for localStorage operations, storage quota management, and recovery from corrupted data scenarios. Provide examples of custom persistence configurations and debugging techniques for state synchronization issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Display Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for tool display components in AIPulse. Document the ToolCard component implementation including dynamic icon rendering, hover states, and interactive elements. Explain the card layout structure with icon area, title section, description display, and action buttons. Detail the drag-and-drop integration using DnD Kit including sortable behavior, visual feedback, and animation transitions. Document the Framer Motion integration for smooth animations, hover effects, and state-based transformations. Include accessibility features such as proper ARIA labels, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support. Address responsive design considerations, dark/light theme adaptation, and cross-browser compatibility. Provide examples of component customization, styling overrides, and integration patterns with other UI components.

wiki_catalogs.name: Animations & Interactive Elements
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AIPulse animation system and interactive elements. Document Framer Motion integration for entrance/exit animations, hover effects, and state transitions. Explain the animation timing, easing functions, and performance optimization techniques. Detail interactive component behaviors including button states, modal transitions, theme switching animations, and drag-and-drop feedback. Include guidelines for creating smooth micro-interactions, managing animation queues, and ensuring accessibility with reduced motion preferences. Document animation composition patterns, trigger conditions, and cross-component animation coordination.

wiki_catalogs.name: UI Components
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed documentation for AIPulse reusable UI components that form the foundation of the application's visual design system. Document Button component with variants, sizes, and interactive states; Input component with validation and styling options; Modal component for overlay dialogs; Select component for dropdown selections; and TextArea component for multi-line text input. Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and styling consistency through Tailwind CSS utilities. Detail the index export pattern for centralized component access and reusability. Include examples of component customization, theme integration, and accessibility features. Document component state management, event handling patterns, and integration with form validation systems. Address component performance optimization and best practices for consistent UI implementation across the application.

wiki_catalogs.name: Application Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create architectural documentation for AIPulse application design and component organization. Describe the high-level component-driven architecture with centralized state management using Zustand. Document the separation of concerns between UI components, state management, data models, and utilities. Explain the store pattern implementation in toolsStore.ts including CRUD operations, category management, and drag-and-drop reordering. Detail the component hierarchy starting from App.tsx as the root component, through layout components (Header, Footer), feature components (CategoryTabs, ToolGrid, SearchBar), and modal components (ToolModal, DeleteModal). Document data flow patterns from user interactions through component events to state updates and automatic persistence. Include architectural patterns such as Provider pattern for state sharing, Factory pattern for component creation, and Observer pattern for reactive updates. Address the integration of external libraries like DnD Kit and Framer Motion within the overall architecture. Explain design decisions, trade-offs, and constraints that shaped the current architecture.

wiki_catalogs.name: Selectors & Performance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for state selectors and performance optimization techniques in AIPulse. Document the getter patterns including getFilteredTools() for category and search filtering with proper sorting, and getRecentlyUsedTools() for recent usage tracking. Explain the selector implementation strategies, memoization patterns, and performance considerations for large datasets. Detail the state derivation techniques, computed properties, and efficient filtering algorithms. Include optimization strategies for drag-and-drop reordering, search debouncing, and component re-render minimization. Document best practices for state subscriptions, selector composition, and avoiding unnecessary re-renders. Provide examples of performance profiling, bottleneck identification, and optimization techniques for smooth user interactions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation and Error Handling
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for validation and error handling in tool management operations. Document form validation rules including required field validation, URL format checking, and duplicate detection. Explain real-time validation feedback, error message display, and form state management. Detail error handling strategies for invalid URLs, network failures, and validation violations. Document user feedback mechanisms including toast notifications, inline error messages, and form field highlighting. Include validation lifecycle from initial input through submission and error resolution. Address edge cases such as empty forms, malformed URLs, and concurrent modification conflicts. Provide examples of validation implementation, error boundary handling, and user experience optimization for error scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: Accessibility & Keyboard Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive accessibility documentation for AIPulse application. Document ARIA attributes implementation, semantic HTML structure, and screen reader support across all components. Explain keyboard navigation patterns, focus management, and tab order optimization. Detail color contrast requirements, text alternatives for icons, and assistive technology compatibility. Include guidelines for managing focus traps in modals, providing meaningful error messages, and supporting keyboard-only operation. Document accessibility testing approaches, automated testing strategies, and manual verification procedures. Address WCAG compliance levels and accessibility audit processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Modal System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive documentation for AIPulse modal system architecture and implementation patterns. Document ToolModal for creating and editing AI tools with form validation, data persistence, and state management integration. Detail DeleteModal for confirmation workflows and destructive action handling. Explain the underlying Modal component base class and its role in the modal architecture. Document modal lifecycle management, z-index stacking, backdrop handling, and keyboard navigation support. Include examples of modal composition patterns, form integration, and error handling strategies. Detail the relationship between modals and the Zustand store for state synchronization. Address accessibility considerations including focus management, ARIA attributes, and screen reader support. Document modal animation systems, transition effects, and performance optimization techniques for smooth user experiences.

wiki_catalogs.name: Component System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive component system documentation for AIPulse UI architecture and reusable component patterns. Document the three-tier component organization: layout components (Header, Footer), feature components (CategoryTabs, ToolGrid, SearchBar, ThemeToggle), and UI components (Button, Input, Modal, Select, TextArea). Explain component composition patterns, prop interfaces, and event handling mechanisms. Detail the component hierarchy starting from App.tsx root, through layout wrappers, to specialized feature implementations. Document the modal system architecture including ToolModal for CRUD operations and DeleteModal for confirmation handling. Include component lifecycle management, state integration with Zustand store, and performance optimization techniques. Address component reusability patterns, styling consistency through Tailwind CSS, and accessibility considerations. Provide guidelines for extending the component system, creating new components, and maintaining design consistency. Document component testing approaches and integration patterns with external libraries.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed state management documentation for AIPulse Zustand implementation and data persistence strategies. Document the toolsStore.ts architecture including state shape definition, action creators for CRUD operations, and selector patterns for efficient re-renders. Explain the ToolsState interface covering tool entities, categories, search filters, and user preferences. Detail the implementation of category-based filtering, search functionality, and drag-and-drop reordering within the store. Document the Zustand middleware integration for automatic localStorage persistence, state serialization/deserialization, and data migration handling. Include state update patterns, batch operations, and performance optimization techniques. Address the relationship between store state and component props, subscription patterns, and state derivation using selectors. Provide examples of common state operations including adding tools, updating categories, and managing user preferences. Document error handling strategies, state validation, and recovery mechanisms for corrupted data.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Models & Types
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive data model documentation for AIPulse type system and entity definitions. Document the AITool interface including properties like id, name, description, url, category, icon, and timestamps. Detail the Category interface defining tool classification system with category names, descriptions, and color schemes. Explain the ToolsState interface encompassing the complete application state including tools array, activeCategory filter, searchQuery, and user preferences. Document the ToolFormData interface for form validation and submission handling. Include type definitions for drag-and-drop operations, modal states, and UI component props. Address TypeScript patterns used throughout the application including optional properties, union types, and generic constraints. Provide examples of data validation, type guards, and runtime type checking. Document the relationship between TypeScript interfaces and runtime data structures, including serialization and deserialization considerations. Include guidelines for extending data models and maintaining type safety across component boundaries.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tool Management System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed tool management documentation for AIPulse CRUD operations and tool lifecycle management. Document the complete tool creation process including form validation, category assignment, and icon selection through ToolModal component. Explain the tool editing workflow with real-time validation, change tracking, and update persistence. Detail the deletion process including confirmation dialogs, cascade effects, and data cleanup. Document the tool launch functionality integrating with external AI tool URLs and browser window management. Include tool organization features such as drag-and-drop reordering, category-based filtering, and search functionality. Address tool metadata management including descriptions, URLs, and categorization. Document the ToolCard component implementation covering individual tool display, interactive elements, and user actions. Provide examples of tool manipulation operations, bulk operations, and data integrity maintenance. Include error handling strategies for invalid URLs, duplicate tools, and validation failures. wiki_catalogs.name: User Interface & Experience
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive UI/UX documentation for AIPulse user interface design and user experience patterns. Document the theme system implementation including dark/light mode toggle, color scheme management, and system preference detection. Explain the responsive design approach using Tailwind CSS utility classes and mobile-first principles. Detail the animation system powered by Framer Motion including entrance/exit animations, hover effects, and transition patterns. wiki_catalogs.name: Styling Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed styling architecture documentation for AIPulse Tailwind CSS implementation and utility-first design system. Document the Tailwind CSS configuration including custom color palette, spacing scale, typography system, and dark mode customization. Explain the clsx and tailwind-merge integration through the cn utility function for conditional class composition. Detail the color system including semantic colors, brand accents, and accessibility-compliant contrast ratios. wiki_catalogs.name: Performance Optimization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive performance optimization documentation for AIPulse application performance improvements and best practices. Document the debounced search implementation using the useDebounce hook for efficient query processing and reduced re-renders. Explain component memoization strategies including React.memo usage, useMemo, and useCallback for expensive computations. Detail the drag-and-drop performance optimization through efficient state updates and minimal re-renders during sorting operations. wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# AIPulse: AI Tool Manager Application

## Project Introduction

AIPulse is a modern, interactive web application designed to help users organize, manage, and discover AI tools efficiently. The platform provides a centralized hub for AI tools across various categories including text generation, image creation, video/audio processing, and development assistance.

### Purpose Statement
The application serves as a personal AI tool manager that allows users to:
- Catalog and organize their favorite AI tools
- Discover new AI tools through categorized browsing
- Maintain a history of recently used tools
- Customize their experience with dark/light themes
- Reorder tools based on usage patterns

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Organization**: Provide intuitive categorization and sorting of AI tools
- **Discoverability**: Enable users to find relevant AI tools quickly
- **Personalization**: Allow customization of tool collections and themes
- **Usability**: Deliver smooth drag-and-drop interactions and responsive design

### Target Audience
- AI enthusiasts and professionals seeking organized tool management
- Developers looking for AI-assisted coding tools
- Content creators utilizing AI for text and media generation
- Researchers exploring various AI applications

## Technical Architecture

The application follows a modern React-based architecture with TypeScript for type safety and Zustand for state management. The system is built with a component-driven approach emphasizing reusability and maintainability.

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[App Component] --> B[Layout Components]
    A --> C[Feature Components]
    A --> D[UI Components]
    A --> E[Modals]
    
    B --> F[Header]
    B --> G[Footer]
    
    C --> H[CategoryTabs]
    C --> I[RecentlyUsed]
    C --> J[ToolGrid]
    
    J --> K[ToolCard]
    K --> L[Drag & Drop Context]
    
    D --> M[Button]
    D --> N[Input]
    D --> O[Select]
    D --> P[Modal]
    
    E --> Q[ToolModal]
    E --> R[DeleteModal]
    
    S[Zustand Store] --> T[Tools State]
    S --> U[Categories State]
    S --> V[UI State]
    
    T --> W[CRUD Operations]
    T --> X[Filtering]
    T --> Y[Reordering]
```

### Component Breakdown

**Layout Components**: Handle application structure and navigation
- Header: Provides branding and action buttons
- Footer: Displays application information and links

**Feature Components**: Core functional modules
- CategoryTabs: Organizes tools by categories with filtering
- RecentlyUsed: Tracks and displays frequently accessed tools
- ToolGrid: Main container for displaying AI tools in a grid layout

**UI Components**: Reusable interface elements
- Button: Consistent button styling across the application
- Input/Select/TextArea: Form controls with consistent design
- Modal: Container for dialog-based interactions

**State Management**: Centralized state handling through Zustand store
- Tools CRUD operations (Create, Read, Update, Delete)
- Category management
- User preferences (theme, filters)
- Recently used tools tracking

### Design Patterns

The application implements several key design patterns:

**Store Pattern**: Zustand provides a clean separation between state logic and UI components
**Provider Pattern**: Context providers wrap components to share state
**Factory Pattern**: Component factories for consistent UI element creation
**Observer Pattern**: Reactive state updates trigger UI re-renders

### System Relationships

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant User
    participant App
    participant Store
    participant API
    
    User->>App: Interact with UI
    App->>Store: Dispatch action
    Store->>Store: Update state
    Store->>App: Notify subscribers
    App->>User: Render updated UI
    
    Note over Store: Persistence middleware
    Store->>API: Save to localStorage
```

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Application Root** (`src/App.tsx`)
- Central orchestrator managing global state and modal interactions
- Implements theme switching and background grid pattern
- Coordinates all feature components and modals

**Entry Point** (`src/main.tsx`)
- React application bootstrap with strict mode enabled
- Mounts the root App component to the DOM

### Core Modules

**State Management** (`src/stores/toolsStore.ts`)
- Implements comprehensive CRUD operations for AI tools
- Manages category organization and filtering
- Handles drag-and-drop reordering with smooth animations
- Persists user data to localStorage for persistence

**Type Definitions** (`src/types/index.ts`)
- Strongly typed interfaces for AI tools and categories
- Defines store actions and state structure
- Ensures type safety across the entire application

**Configuration** (`vite.config.ts`, `tailwind.config.js`)
- Vite configuration with custom path aliases for clean imports
- Tailwind CSS setup with custom color schemes and animations
- Dark mode support through CSS classes

### Configuration Approach

**Build Configuration** (`vite.config.ts`)
- Custom path aliases for cleaner import statements
- React plugin integration for JSX/TSX support
- Development server configuration

**Styling Configuration** (`tailwind.config.js`)
- Dark mode implementation using class-based approach
- Custom color palette with primary and background variations
- Animation system with custom keyframes and transitions
- Responsive design utilities for mobile-first approach

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework** (`package.json`)
- React 19.2.4: Component-based UI library
- TypeScript: Type safety and enhanced developer experience
- Vite: Fast build tool and development server

**State Management** (`zustand@^5.0.11`)
- Lightweight state management solution
- No boilerplate required for complex state logic
- Built-in persistence middleware for data retention

**UI & Interaction** (`framer-motion@^12.35.0`)
- Smooth animations and transitions throughout the application
- Motion components for enhanced user experience
- Performance-optimized animations

**Drag & Drop** (`@dnd-kit/core@^6.3.1`)
- Advanced drag-and-drop functionality for tool reordering
- Multi-platform support (mouse, touch, keyboard)
- Accessibility-compliant interactions

### Integration Points

**Component Communication** (`src/components/features/ToolGrid.tsx`)
- DnD Kit integration for drag-and-drop reordering
- Framer Motion for smooth animations during interactions
- Lucide React icons for consistent visual language

**Persistence Layer** (`src/stores/toolsStore.ts`)
- LocalStorage integration through Zustand middleware
- Automatic serialization/deserialization of complex data structures
- Partial state persistence for optimal performance

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[App] --> B[Header]
    A --> C[Main Content]
    A --> D[Footer]
    
    C --> E[CategoryTabs]
    C --> F[RecentlyUsed]
    C --> G[ToolGrid]
    
    G --> H[ToolCard]
    H --> I[DragHandle]
    H --> J[ToolActions]
    
    E --> K[CategoryFilter]
    F --> L[RecentToolsList]
    
    M[Zustand Store] --> N[Tools State]
    M --> O[UI State]
    M --> P[Preferences]
    
    N --> Q[CRUD Operations]
    O --> R[Theme Management]
    P --> S[Filter State]
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Tool Management System**
- Complete CRUD operations for AI tools with validation
- Drag-and-drop reordering with visual feedback
- Category-based organization with dynamic filtering
- Search functionality across tool properties

**User Experience Enhancements**
- Smooth animations using Framer Motion for transitions
- Dark/light theme toggle with system preference detection
- Recently used tools tracking with automatic updates
- Responsive design supporting all device sizes

**Data Persistence**
- Automatic localStorage synchronization
- Schema migration support for future updates
- Optimized storage with selective state persistence

### Implementation Highlights

**Advanced State Management** (`src/stores/toolsStore.ts`)
- Comprehensive tool lifecycle management
- Complex filtering and sorting algorithms
- Efficient state updates with minimal re-renders
- Type-safe action dispatching and state updates

**Interactive Grid System** (`src/components/features/ToolGrid.tsx`)
- DnD Kit integration for sophisticated drag-and-drop
- Dynamic grid layout with responsive breakpoints
- Empty state handling with contextual messaging
- Performance optimization for large datasets

**Category Organization** (`src/components/features/CategoryTabs.tsx`)
- Dynamic tab generation from tool categories
- Visual indicators for tool counts per category
- Animated active state transitions
- Intelligent category filtering

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Loading
    Loading --> Idle: Data Loaded
    Idle --> Searching: User Types
    Idle --> Filtering: Category Selected
    Searching --> Results: Matches Found
    Searching --> Empty: No Matches
    Filtering --> Results: Tools Found
    Filtering --> Empty: No Tools
    Results --> Dragging: Drag Started
    Dragging --> Sorting: Drag Completed
    Sorting --> Idle: State Updated
    Empty --> Adding: Add Tool Clicked
    Adding --> Idle: Tool Added
    Idle --> Editing: Edit Tool Clicked
    Editing --> Idle: Changes Saved
    Idle --> Deleting: Delete Tool Clicked
    Deleting --> Idle: Tool Removed
```

**Performance Optimization Features**
- Memoized computations for filtered tool lists
- Lazy loading for large tool collections
- Efficient drag-and-drop with virtualization
- Debounced search input for improved responsiveness

**Accessibility Features**
- Keyboard navigation support for all interactive elements
- Screen reader compatibility for assistive technologies
- High contrast mode support for visual accessibility
- Focus management for modal interactions

---

**Sources:**
- [package.json](package.json)
- [App.tsx](src/App.tsx)
- [toolsStore.ts](src/stores/toolsStore.ts)
- [defaultTools.ts](src/constants/defaultTools.ts)
- [index.ts](src/types/index.ts)
- [vite.config.ts](vite.config.ts)
- [tailwind.config.js](tailwind.config.js)
- [ToolGrid.tsx](src/components/features/ToolGrid.tsx)
- [CategoryTabs.tsx](src/components/features/CategoryTabs.tsx)
- [main.tsx](src/main.tsx)
- [Button.tsx](src/components/ui/Button.tsx)

</blog>
